title: Iron Tower
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Structure |image=[[File:IronTower-TheReturnOfBenKenobi.png]] |name=Iron Tower |constructed= |destroyed=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]"[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]" {{C|partially}}{{CSWECite|II|116|Iron Tower}} |type= |location=[[Aridus/Legends|Aridus]] |interest= |rebuilt= |builder=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' |height= |width= |affiliation=Galactic Empire }} {{Quote|The Iron Tower is the greatest symbol of Imperial tyranny on Aridus! While it operates…the Chubbits are being steadily destroyed!|An Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator|}} The '''Iron Tower''' was an automated [[power transformer]] and [[signal amplifier]] constructed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aridus/Legends|Aridus]] to overcome the intense interference generated by its ionized [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. However, its powerful emanations had crippling effects on the [[nervous system]]s of the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Chubbit]]s, forcing them to [[Chubbit resistance|take up arms]] against the Empire. It was [[Battle at the Iron Tower|disabled and partially destroyed]] as a result of the sacrifice of a [[Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator|man]] impersonating [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who was [[Death/Legends|fatally injured]] by the ensuing explosion. ==Description== {{Quote|Atmospheric conditions on Aridus make long-range communication impossible…''"''
''"''The Iron Tower is something the Empire built to overcome that, Ben?''"''
''"''It's a gigantic power transformer and signal amplifier, Luke… totally automated. Without it… their hovertrain ore transport system couldn't function. But the ultra-strong frequency of its signals has a devastating effect on the native Chubbits… crippling their nervous systems… in time, fatally!|An Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator and Luke Skywalker|}} The Iron Tower was an automated structure that functioned as a massive [[power transformer]] and [[signal amplifier]], allowing the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] unrestricted communications and supplying energy to run its [[Hovertrain/Legends|hovertrain]]{{CSWECite|I|41|Aridus}} ore transport system. Its control chamber could be accessed via [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] or emergency stairwell. While the Iron Tower had automated defenses, including deathtrap mechanisms such as death beams, it was also protected by [[Imperial patrol speeder (repulsortank)|Imperial patrol speeders]]. The area immediately surrounding the tower was the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aridus/Legends|Aridus]]' [[Mining/Legends|mining]] region, the location of hazardous [[Magma/Legends|lava]] pits. ==History== [[File:Iron_Tower_explosion.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The upper levels of the Iron Tower are destroyed]] {{Quote|The Iron Tower was a ''trap'' set by Darth Vader…but this man caused it to ''backfire''!|Luke Skywalker, in reference to an Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator|}} When Imperial [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] located valuable natural resources on Aridus, Imperial mining teams rushed to exploit them. However, the ionized [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] resulting from the planet's magnetic fluctuations generated intense interference that wreaked havoc with the tunneling equipment and made all long-range communications impossible. The mining [[Foreman/Legends|foreman]] pleaded with [[Gender/Legends|his]] superiors to authorize the construction of a massive power transformer and signal amplifier; otherwise, operations on Aridus would have to be scrapped. Anxious to protect his investment, the [[Narvath sector/Legends|Narvath sector's]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] approved construction of the Iron Tower, a monumental structure that could punch its signal through any interference. Mining resumed, and Imperial hovertrains began ferrying workers to the dig sites. However, the Imperials never stopped to consider what effect the Iron Tower might have on the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Chubbit]]s. They quietly watched as the Empire ravaged their world, but the new structure was a [[Death/Legends|lethal]] threat. Its ultra-strong signal frequencies had crippling effects on the Chubbit [[nervous system]], and they were forced to [[Chubbit resistance|take up arms]] for their continued survival, while the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] gladly supplied weapons to their cause. In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],{{DB|character|obiwankenobi|Kenobi, Obi-Wan (Ben)}} the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] sent a trained [[Actor/Legends|actor]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi impersonator|impersonating]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who Vader had [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|killed]] aboard the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], to take control of the Chubbit resistance as part of a plot to capture [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Vader had the impostor lure Skywalker to the Iron Tower, under the guise of [[Battle at the Iron Tower|destroying]] the structure, which the impostor described as "the greatest symbol of Imperial tyranny on Aridus". Once there, the actor was ordered by Vader to meet him in the control chamber in order to lure Skywalker there by pretending to be hurt. However, the impersonator refused due to his respect for Kenobi and pulled a control level, the tower's overload circuits, to start a catastrophic overload spiral in the structure's upper levels, but not before summoning Skywalker's [[Rebel smuggling ship|ship]] with an [[Distress beacon/Legends|emergency directional beacon]]. He and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were caught in the resulting explosion, which disabled and partially destroyed the Iron Tower. Skywalker arrived in the control chamber after the destruction ceased and cradled the fatally injured man in his arms as he confessed that he was just an impostor who came to love his part. After the impersonator's death, Skywalker's ship arrived and he departing Aridus. Meanwhile, the blast nearly killed Vader, who was rendered unconscious for a period of [[Time/Legends|time]]. He was found by [[Wrenga Jixton]], a former Imperial [[Gunnery Sergeant]] who befriended the local Chubbits while in hiding from the Empire on Aridus, who brought him aboard his [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Knowing that Vader's death would only draw more Imperial forces to the planet, he offered the Sith a deal; in exchange for keeping the Empire away from Aridus, Jixton would do anything he commanded, which Vader accepted.{{GalaxyCite|10|Shadow Stalker}} ==Behind the scenes== The Iron Tower was originally created for the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars (comic strip)|newspaper comic strip]] ''[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]'', written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and penciled by [[Al Williamson]], which ran from [[May 17]] to [[July 25]], [[1982]]. ==Appearances== *"[[The Return of Ben Kenobi]]" {{Reprint|The Return of Ben Kenobi}} {{1st}} *{{GalaxyCite|10|Shadow Stalker}} {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|obiwankenobi|Kenobi, Obi-Wan (Ben)}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Iron Tower }} [[Category:Aridus locations]] [[Category:Buildings]] [[Category:Galactic Empire locations]]
